parameters.display_name: SOC Trace Hub Memory Region 0 buffer Size
parameters.description: Select size of memory region 0 buffer. Memory allocated by BIOS only applies to ITH tool running on the host. For ITH tool running on the target, choose None/OS, memory shall be allocated by tool. User should be cautious to choose the amount of memory. If chosen size is larger than half of system memory, setup will automatically rollback to default value. 0x00:1MB, 0x03:8MB, 0x06:64MB, 0x07:128MB, 0x08:256MB, 0x09:512MB, 0x0A:1GB, 0x0B:2GB, 0x0C:4GB, 0x0D:8GB, 0x0E:0MB

Defines the per-core HT disable mask where: 1 - Disable selected logical core HT, 0 - is ignored. Input is in HEX and each bit maps to a logical core. Ex. A value of '1F' would disable HT for cores 4,3,2,1 and 0. Default is 0, all cores have HT enabled. Range is 0 - 0x7F for max 8 cores. You can only disable up to MAX_CORE_COUNT - 1.

When the threshold is 0, static PVD ratio is selected based on the PVD Mode for SOC. 0: Default.
Array of PVD Mode. Value from 0 to 3 for SOC/CPU. 0x0 = div-1 (VCO = Output clock), 0x1 = div-2 (VCO = 2x Output clock), 0x2 = div-4 (VCO = 4x Output clock), 0x3 = div-8 (VCO = 8x Output clock).

Select FLL Mode Value from 0 to 3. 0x0 = no overclocking, 0x1 = ratio overclocking with nominal (0.5-1x) reference clock frequency, 0x2 = BCLK overclocking with elevated (1-3x) reference clock frequency, 0x3 = BCLK overclocking with extreme elevated (3-5x) reference clock frequency and ratio limited to 63.

parameters.display_name: Package Long duration turbo mode power limit
parameters.description: Power Limit 1 in Milli Watts. BIOS will round to the nearest 1/8W when programming. Value set 120 = 15W. 0 = no custom override. Overclocking SKU: Value must be between Max and Min Power Limits. Other SKUs: This value must be between Min Power Limit and Processor Base Power (TDP) Limit. If value is 0, BIOS will program Processor Base Power (TDP) value. Units are based on POWER_MGMT_CONFIG.CustomPowerUnit. Valid Range 0 to 32767.

parameters.display_name: Package Short duration turbo mode power limit
parameters.description: Power Limit 2 in Milli Watts. BIOS will round to the nearest 1/8W when programming. Value set 120 = 15W. If the value is 0, BIOS will program this value as 1.25*Processor Base Power (TDP). Processor applies control policies such that the package power does not exceed this limit. Units are based on POWER_MGMT_CONFIG.CustomPowerUnit. Valid Range 0 to 32767.

Power Limit 3 in Milli Watts. BIOS will round to the nearest 1/8W when programming. Value set 120 = 15W. XE SKU: Any value can be programmed. Overclocking SKU: Value must be between Max and Min Power Limits. Other SKUs: This value must be between Min Power Limit and Processor Base Power (TDP) Limit. If the value is 0, BIOS leaves the hardware default value. Units are based on POWER_MGMT_CONFIG.CustomPowerUnit. Valid Range 0 to 32767.

Power Limit 4 in Milli Watts. BIOS will round to the nearest 1/8W when programming. Value set 120 = 15W. If the value is 0, BIOS leaves default value. Units are based on POWER_MGMT_CONFIG.CustomPowerUnit. Valid Range 0 to 32767.
Configure Power Limit 4 Boost in Watts. Valid Range 0 to 1023 in step size of 1 Watt. The value 0 means disable.

Units are based on POWER_MGMT_CONFIG.CustomPowerUnit. Valid Range 0 to 32767.

Units are based on POWER_MGMT_CONFIG.CustomPowerUnit. Valid Range 0 to 32767.

parameters.display_name: Long term Power Limit value for custom cTDP level 2

Processor applies control policies such that the package power does not exceed this limit. Units are based on POWER_MGMT_CONFIG.CustomPowerUnit. Valid Range 0 to 32767.

parameters.display_name: Short term Power Limit value for custom cTDP level 3

Overclocking SKU: Value must be between Max and Min Power Limits. Other SKUs: This value must be between Min Power Limit and Processor Base Power (TDP) Limit. Units are based on POWER_MGMT_CONFIG.CustomPowerUnit. Valid Range 0 to 32767.

Processor applies control policies such that the package power does not exceed this limit. Units are based on POWER_MGMT_CONFIG.CustomPowerUnit. Valid Range 0 to 32767.

Platform Power Limit 1 Power in Milli Watts. BIOS will round to the nearest 1/8W when programming. Value set 120 = 15W. Any value can be programmed between Max and Min Power Limits. This setting will act as the new PL1 value for the Package RAPL algorithm. Units are based on POWER_MGMT_CONFIG.CustomPowerUnit. Valid Range 0 to 32767.

Platform Power Limit 2 Power in Milli Watts. BIOS will round to the nearest 1/8W when programming. Value set 120 = 15W. Any value can be programmed between Max and Min Power Limits. This setting will act as the new Max Turbo Power (PL2) value for the Package RAPL algorithm. Units are based on POWER_MGMT_CONFIG.CustomPowerUnit. Valid Range 0 to 32767.

parameters.display_name: Pvd Ratio Threshold for SOC/CPU die
parameters.description: Array of Pvd Ratio Threshold for SOC/CPU die is the threshold value for input ratio (P0 to Pn) to select the multiplier so that the output is within the DCO frequency range. As per the die selected, this threshold is applied to SA and MC/CMI PLL for SOC die and SA, Ring and Atom PLL for CPU die. Range 0-63. When the threshold is 0, static PVD ratio is selected based on the PVD Mode for SOC. 0: Default.
Array of PVD Mode. Value from 0 to 3 for SOC/CPU. 0x0 = div-1 (VCO = Output clock), 0x1 = div-2 (VCO = 2x Output clock), 0x2 = div-4 (VCO = 4x Output clock), 0x3 = div-8 (VCO = 8x Output clock).

Select FLL Mode Value from 0 to 3. 0x0 = no overclocking, 0x1 = ratio overclocking with nominal (0.5-1x) reference clock frequency, 0x2 = BCLK overclocking with elevated (1-3x) reference clock frequency, 0x3 = BCLK overclocking with extreme elevated (3-5x) reference clock frequency and ratio limited to 63.

parameters.display_name: Package Long duration turbo mode power limit
parameters.description: Power Limit 1 in Milli Watts. BIOS will round to the nearest 1/8W when programming. Value set 120 = 15W. 0 = no custom override. Overclocking SKU: Value must be between Max and Min Power Limits. Other SKUs: This value must be between Min Power Limit and Processor Base Power (TDP) Limit. If value is 0, BIOS will program Processor Base Power (TDP) value. Units are based on POWER_MGMT_CONFIG.CustomPowerUnit. Valid Range 0 to 32767.

parameters.display_name: Package Short duration turbo mode power limit
parameters.description: Power Limit 2 in Milli Watts. BIOS will round to the nearest 1/8W when programming. Value set 120 = 15W. If the value is 0, BIOS will program this value as 1.25*Processor Base Power (TDP). Processor applies control policies such that the package power does not exceed this limit. Units are based on POWER_MGMT_CONFIG.CustomPowerUnit. Valid Range 0 to 32767.

Power Limit 3 in Milli Watts. BIOS will round to the nearest 1/8W when programming. Value set 120 = 15W. XE SKU: Any value can be programmed. Overclocking SKU: Value must be between Max and Min Power Limits. Other SKUs: This value must be between Min Power Limit and Processor Base Power (TDP) Limit. If the value is 0, BIOS leaves the hardware default value. Units are based on POWER_MGMT_CONFIG.CustomPowerUnit. Valid Range 0 to 32767.

Power Limit 4 in Milli Watts. BIOS will round to the nearest 1/8W when programming. Value set 120 = 15W. If the value is 0, BIOS leaves default value. Units are based on POWER_MGMT_CONFIG.CustomPowerUnit. Valid Range 0 to 32767.
Configure Power Limit 4 Boost in Watts. Valid Range 0 to 1023 in step size of 1 Watt. The value 0 means disable.

parameters.display_name: Enable or Disable Per Core P State OS control
parameters.description: Enable/Disable Per Core P state OS control mode. When set, the highest core request is used for all other core requests. 0: Disable; 1: Enable $EN_DIS

parameters.display_name: Enable or Disable HwP Autonomous Per Core P State OS control
parameters.description: Disable Autonomous PCPS Autonomous will request the same value for all cores all the time. 0: Disable; 1: Enable $EN_DIS

parameters.display_name: Enable or Disable HwP Autonomous EPP Grouping
parameters.description: Enable EPP grouping Autonomous will request the same values for all cores with same EPP. Disable EPP grouping autonomous will not necessarily request same values for all cores with same EPP.  0: Disable ; 1: Enable $EN_DIS

Applies cTDP(Assured Power) initialization settings based on non-cTDP(Assured Power) or cTDP(Assured Power). Default is 1: Applies to cTDP(Assured Power); if 0 then applies non-cTDP(Assured Power) and BIOS will bypass cTDP(Assured Power) initialzation flow $EN_DIS
Enable/Disable HWP Lock support in Misc Power Management MSR. 0: Disable, 1: Enable  $EN_DIS
Enable Dual Tau Boost feature. This is only applicable for Desktop 35W/65W/125W sku. When DPTF is enabled this feature is ignored. 0: Disable; 1: Enable $EN_DIS

Use Response Mode to adjust Psys_PL3 power reduction behavior. Battery-enabled systems use Gradual Power Reduction. 0: Gradual Power Reduction ; 1: Aggressive Power Reduction 0: Gradual Power Reduction, 1: Aggressive Power Reduction
Option to disable Power Floor Managment for SOC. Disabling this might effectively raise power floor of the SoC and may lead to stability issues. 0: Disable, 1: Enable  $EN_DIS
SoC can disconnect secondary/external display to lower SoC floor power (Default enabled).  0: Disable: Display disconnect will not be used by SoC., 1: Enable  $EN_DIS
Enable/Disable Resource Priority Feature. Enable/Disable;  0: Disable ; 1:Enable $EN_DIS
SoC can downgrade PCIe gen speed to lower SoC floor power (Default enabled).  0: Disable: Reduction in PCIe gen speed will not be used by SoC., 1: Enable  $EN_DIS
Reserved for CPU Post-Mem Test $EN_DIS
Test, Send End of Post message. Disable(0x0): Disable EOP message, Send in PEI(0x1): EOP send in PEI, Send in DXE(0x2)(Default): EOP send in DXE 0:Disable, 1:Send in PEI, 2:Send in DXE, 3:Reserved
Test, 0: disable, 1: enable, Setting this option disables setting D0I3 bit for all HECI devices $EN_DIS
Test, Determine if MCTP Broadcast is enabled 0: Disable; 1: Enable. $EN_DIS

parameters.display_name: Low Power Mode Enable/Disable config mask

Performance-core Turbo Ratio Limit Ratio0-7 (TRLR) defines the turbo ratio (max is 85 in normal mode and 120 in core extension mode). Ratio[0]: This Turbo Ratio Limit Ratio0 must be greater than or equal all other ratio values. If this value is invalid, thn set all other active cores to minimum. Otherwise, align the Ratio Limit to 0. Please check each active cores. Ratio[1~7]: This Turbo Ratio Limit Ratio1 must be <= to Turbo Ratio Limit Ratio0~6.

parameters.display_name: Turbo Ratio Limit Num Core array
parameters.description: Performance-core Turbo Ratio Limit Core0~7 defines the core range, the turbo ratio is defined in Turbo Ratio Limit Ratio0~7. If value is zero, this entry is ignored.

parameters.display_name: ATOM Turbo Ratio Limit Ratio array
parameters.description: Efficient-core Turbo Ratio Limit Ratio0-7 defines the turbo ratio (max is 85 irrespective of the core extension mode), the core range is defined in E-core Turbo Ratio Limit CoreCount0-7.

parameters.display_name: ATOM Turbo Ratio Limit Num Core array
parameters.description: Efficient-core Turbo Ratio Limit CoreCount0-7 defines the core range, the turbo ratio is defined in E-core Turbo Ratio Limit Ratio0-7. If value is zero, this entry is ignored.
